name: Millennial Decay
white: Knees that crack like glow sticks.
Throwing my back out mid-sneeze.
The pure erotic rush of canceled plans.
Canceling plans I made with myself.
A houseplant named after the child I'm not having.
The houseplant hospice on my windowsill.
A gym membership that functions as a guilt subscription.
The gym bag that has been fermenting in my trunk all year.
A two-beer hangover with a three-business-day recovery arc.
Being tired in a load-bearing way.
The involuntary noise I make sitting down.
The worse noise I make standing back up.
A mortgage, a mythical creature like the phoenix.
Avocado toast, the official breakfast of never owning property.
The group chat that has been scheduling one dinner since March.
A side hustle for my side hustle.
Burnout as a personality.
Using the word "adulting" and hating myself immediately.
A little treat, as a coping mechanism and a lifestyle.
9 PM, also known as millennial midnight.
Sexting my spouse from the other end of the same couch.
Scheduled spontaneity.
Foreplay that begins with quad stretches.
Pulling a hamstring in missionary position.
My chiropractor, who has seen me more than my friends have.
Checking my credit score like it's a horoscope.
A 401(k) with $61 in it.
Retirement, a bedtime story banks tell.
A meal-kit subscription that decomposes into guilt every Thursday.
The one pan I actually use.
An air fryer that repaired my will to live.
Getting into bed at 8:45 like a Victorian ghost.
Mouth tape, blackout curtains, a sound machine, and despair.
Injuring myself by sleeping.
The thing I can't come to your thing because of, which is nothing.
Protecting my nothing time at all costs.
Rescheduling brunch until everyone involved is dead.
My back, which goes out more than I do.
A knee that can predict rain.
Compression socks, removed seductively.
Newfound, unstoppable enthusiasm for fiber.
Getting genuinely aroused by a good night's sleep.
Maintenance sex on clean-sheet day.
Sex quiet enough not to wake the dog.
The dog, our firstborn.
An emotional support water bottle the size of a toddler.
Declaring bankruptcy on my inbox.
A meeting that could have been an email.
Typing "No worries!" with tears in my eyes.
The exclamation points keeping my coworkers from knowing I'm dead inside.
Crying in a parked car as me-time.
Paying my landlord's mortgage for him.
A landlord who painted over the light switches.
A starter home priced like a superyacht.
Real-estate listings, viewed the way I once viewed porn.
A birthday party at a brewery that ends at 7 PM sharp.
Saying "ope" while edging past a stranger.
The first gray pube, and the long silence that followed.
Loudly declaring that this bar is too loud.
New Year's Eve, asleep by 10:15.
A hip that clicks in 4/4 time.
Stretching before, during, and after sex.
An ergonomic desk chair that costs more than my first car.
Anti-inflammatories, taken recreationally.
The specific groan of a millennial exiting a couch.
A weighted blanket and no further questions.
Splitting eleven streaming subscriptions like a custody arrangement.
My ex's streaming password, our last surviving intimacy.
A garage shrine of abandoned hobby equipment.
A sourdough starter with abandonment issues.
Whispering "per my last email" like a curse.
Doomscrolling until my arm falls asleep.
An eleven-step skincare routine with zero visible results.
Melatonin and a podcast about murder.
Falling asleep during the sex I scheduled.
Student loans old enough to have their own student loans.
A bachelorette party that requires a group nap.
Attempting a new position and hearing a sound like a dry branch snapping.
Bringing a jacket, just in case.
The good scissors, which no one may touch.

Being an adult is mostly lying awake at 3 AM thinking about _.
I canceled plans again this week to stay home with _.
This year, self-care means _.
